name: R1-1776
note: MIT post-train of DeepSeek-R1 removing CCP censorship on ~300 topics via 40K-prompt multilingual SFT with NeMo 2.0; not currently listed on Perplexity's hosted API pricing or HF Inference Providers.
tpsNote: 671B MoE far exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB even at Q4 (~335GB needed); community low-bit GGUF (e.g. Unsloth Q2_K_XL) needs multi-GPU/CPU offload.

results.notes: AIME 2024 pass@1, relaying Perplexity's own launch benchmark chart (original R1-1776 base model scored 80.96 on the same eval)

verificationNotes: Existence, Feb 18 2025 release (confirmed via perplexity.ai/de/hub/blog/open-sourcing-r1-1776 publish-date metadata), MIT license, and 671B/37B MoE DeepSeek-R1 architecture all confirmed. Dropped the sole lineage-bench result row (0.205 vs DeepSeek-R1's 0.780): follow-up investigation of the same Reddit/HuggingFace discussion thread shows the original low score was attributed to a flawed multi-threaded benchmark harness, and a corrected re-run had 'R1-1776 climbed to first place' — the 0.205 figure is a retracted/unreliable artifact, not a valid benchmark result, so it was removed rather than kept as a misleading number.
